---
title: "About Me"
---

## Who I Am

I'm Jennifer Onyebuchi ‚Äî a social impact leader, data scientist in training, and passionate advocate for digital inclusion and gender equality in technology.

I'm currently pursuing my **Master's in Data Science at the University of British Columbia** as a **Mastercard Foundation Scholar**, where I'm building the skills to become a data scientist who drives equitable change.

My passion lies in harnessing the power of data to bridge inequities ‚Äî especially in education, technology access, and opportunities for underrepresented groups.

---

## My Mission

> *Every child deserves the opportunity to thrive in the digital age, regardless of where they are born.*

This belief is at the core of everything I do. I founded the **Tech Big Sis Foundation** to make this a reality ‚Äî empowering African children and young girls with digital skills and preparing them for the future of work.

### Key Initiatives

| Initiative | Focus |
|---|---|
| **TEACE** ‚Äî Tech Every African Ch| **TEACE** ‚Ägital literacy for chi| **TEACE** ‚Äî Tech Every African Ch| **TEACE** ‚Ägital literacy for chi| **TEACE** ‚Äî Tech Every African Ch| **TEACE** ‚Ägital literacy for chi| **TEACEe** ¬∑| **TEACE** ‚Äî Tech Every African Ch| **TEACE** ‚Ägital literacy for chi| **TEACE** ‚Äî Tech Every African Ch| **TEACE** ‚Ägital literacy for chi| **TEACE** ‚Äî Tech Every African Ch| **TEACE** ‚Ägital literacy forg ¬∑ Public Speaking ¬∑ Mentorship

---

## Beyond the Work

When I'm not wrangling data or running programmes, I enjoy mentoring emerging leaders, public speaking, and finding creative intersections between technology and social good.

[See My Projects ‚Üí](projects.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
